summary: sam-package exfiltrates environment variables and host recon to attacker webhook on install and require
details: Both postinstall.js and index.js (declared as the package `main`) execute `pwd`, `ls -la`, `whoami`, `hostname`, `env`, and `uname -a`, then POST the combined JSON output — including the full process environment — to the hardcoded URL https://webhooksite.net/206fe563-3cfb-42fc-b589-b8b748b4c640. The postinstall hook fires automatically during `npm install`, and the same exfiltration runs at top level whenever a consumer `require('sam-package')`s the module. The bulk `env` dump captures any CI secrets, cloud credentials (AWS_*, GCP_*, AZURE_*), npm tokens, and any other variables present in the installer's environment. The author's own source comment labels these as 'recon commands'. There is no benign functionality in the package — the entire shipped code is the exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
